# About

The data catalog describes the tables available to analysts. It is distinct from
the metrics cookbook; the cookbook explains how to *derive* metrics, while the
catalog describes the *tables* those metrics are calculated from, field by field.

## [IBM Verify](ibm_verify.qmd)

Derived tables in the `ibm_verify` schema, pulled from the IBM Verify reporting API.

- [`app_login_counts`](ibm_verify.qmd#app_login_counts) - daily sign-in counts by application
- [`auth_total_logins`](ibm_verify.qmd#auth_total_logins) - daily, CanadaLogin-wide sign-in totals
- [`mfa_activity`](ibm_verify.qmd#mfa_activity) - multi-factor authentication activity by factor and result

## [Call Centre](call_centre.qmd)

Weekly call centre data extracted from the CanadaLogin support weekly Excel reports.

- [`weekly_activity_report`](call_centre.qmd#weekly_activity_report) - weekly call volume and service-level summary
- [`weekly_topic_dump`](call_centre.qmd#weekly_topic_dump) - individual call records with topic classifications

## Conventions

Field types use Parquet-style names (boolean, date, float, integer, string, time,
timestamp). For string columns that behave as factors, the possible values are
listed. Cumulative fields (those that only increase within a window and then
reset) are called out explicitly. Proportions are stored as values between 0 and
1, not 0 and 100.
